_comment: Declarative model selection policy for the intent layer (project 08). Data, not code — editing this file is not a deploy. Models are referenced by stable alias, never a pinned version ID. See docs/projects/08-intent-layer/spec.md (the 'Model selection policy' section) and src/intent/model-policy.ts.

models._comment: Anthropic Opus 4.8 via the Claude CLI alias. Also the judgment-role model for the product-team workflow (project 14) — PM, tech lead, reviewer, designer — replacing the earlier Fable 5 binding.

Codex executor for Layer 5 cross-model dispatch (project 08, A6.1). Provider openai; capability 'coding' to satisfy the Evaluator-role binding once roleDefaults.evaluator is set (A6.2) and the evaluatorDistinctFromGenerator constraint is flipped on (A6.3). Status 'preferred' marks Codex as the intended Evaluator on autonomous engine runs.

Project 14 Phase 8: the artifact-role executor for the product-team workflow — QA test authoring and coder. OpenAI GPT-5.5 via the Codex CLI (`codex exec -m gpt-5.5`). Distinct provider from the reviewer's `opus` by construction (independence gate).

_roleDefaults_comment: Per-role default model alias. `evaluator: codex` (project 08, A6.2) makes the autonomous Evaluator pick a cross-provider model by default so cross-model adjudication is the policy-driven shape. The six product-team roles (project 14, Phase 8) bind per the spec's model table: judgment roles → opus (anthropic, Opus 4.8), artifact roles → gpt-5.5 (openai); the resolver enforces reviewer-vs-coder provider distinctness at resolution time (fail-closed).
